ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 (ScarCruft) ROKRAT C2 via legitimate cloud-storage APIs (pCloud / Yandex / Dropbox / Box)
ucs.description: Hunts ROKRAT / DOGCALL beaconing — APT37 has used pCloud, Yandex Cloud, Dropbox and Box.com REST APIs as a C2 channel for years (a near-signature behaviour first reported by Cisco Talos and Volexity/InkySquid). The detection fires when a non-browser, non-sync-client process — especially a LOLBin or a binary executing from %APPDATA%/%TEMP% — reaches one of these cloud APIs, which is the load-bearing TTP that distinguishes ScarCruft from other DPRK clusters (Lazarus / Kimsuky rarely use this exact channel).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest IN ("api.pcloud.com","eapi.pcloud.com","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","cloud-api.yandex.com","upload.box.com","api.box.com") OR All_Traffic.dest_host IN ("api.pcloud.com","eapi.pcloud.com","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","cloud-api.yandex.com","upload.box.com","api.box.com")) AND NOT All_Traffic.app IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","Dropbox.exe","OneDrive.exe","YandexDisk.exe","Box.exe","BoxEdit.exe","BoxSync.exe","pCloud.exe") by host All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | join type=inner host process_name [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Public\\*") OR Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe")) by host Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | table firstTime lastTime host src user app process_name process_path dest dest_port bytes_out | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let _ROKRATCloudHosts = dynamic(["api.pcloud.com","eapi.pcloud.com","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","cloud-api.yandex.com","upload.box.com","api.box.com"]);
let _LegitClients = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","dropbox.exe","onedrive.exe","yandexdisk.exe","box.exe","boxedit.exe","boxsync.exe","pcloud.exe"]);
let _SuspectPaths = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\", @"\Users\Public\"]);
let _LolBins = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (_ROKRATCloudHosts)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_LegitClients)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_SuspectPaths)
     or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_LolBins)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _ROKRATCloudHosts = dynamic(["api.pcloud.com","eapi.pcloud.com","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","cloud-api.yandex.com","upload.box.com","api.box.com"]);
let _LegitClients = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","dropbox.exe","onedrive.exe","yandexdisk.exe","box.exe","boxedit.exe","boxsync.exe","pcloud.exe"]);
let _LolBins = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe"]);
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where isnotempty(Url) or isnotempty(DstHostname)
| where (Url has_any (_ROKRATCloudHosts)) or (DstHostname in~ (_ROKRATCloudHosts))
| extend ProcName = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where isnotempty(ProcName)
| where ProcName !in (_LegitClients)
| where ProcName in (_LolBins)
     or ActingProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\")
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, SrcUsername, ActingProcessName,
          ActingProcessCommandLine, Url, DstHostname, DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT37 ROKRAT C2 via Legitimate Cloud Storage APIs
id: 7c3a1b1c-4e8d-4a02-8b45-9d2f37c90a01
status: experimental
description: Detects ROKRAT/DOGCALL bidirectional C2 over pCloud, Yandex, Dropbox, and Box public APIs from non-browser/non-sync processes. Highly characteristic of APT37 (ScarCruft / Ricochet Chollima / InkySquid).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0067/
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/rokrat-reloaded/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.g0067
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_dst:
    DestinationHostname:
      - 'api.pcloud.com'
      - 'eapi.pcloud.com'
      - 'content.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'api.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'cloud-api.yandex.net'
      - 'cloud-api.yandex.com'
      - 'upload.box.com'
      - 'api.box.com'
  filter_browsers:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\brave.exe'
      - '\iexplore.exe'
      - '\Dropbox.exe'
      - '\OneDrive.exe'
      - '\YandexDisk.exe'
      - '\Box.exe'
      - '\BoxEdit.exe'
      - '\pCloud.exe'
  condition: selection_dst and not filter_browsers
falsepositives:
  - Custom backup or sync scripts using these cloud APIs (allowlist by hash/path)
  - Developer tools embedding the SDKs of these providers
level: high
ucs.rationale: ROKRAT's defining trait — and the reason Talos coined the family — is its abuse of pCloud/Yandex/Dropbox/Box public APIs as a bidirectional C2 (T1102.002), a behaviour APT37 has retained across the 2017→2024 ROKRAT/DOGCALL/CHINOTTO/M2RAT lineage. Pinning the detection to a curated host-list AND requiring the client process to be either a LOLBin or live in a user-writable path strips the noise from

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.

ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 HWP/Hancom-Office spawning script interpreters or LOLBins (Ricochet Chollima initial-access chain)
ucs.description: ScarCruft is one of the only major APT clusters that weaponises HWP/HWPX (Hangul Office Word Processor) documents, exploiting Hancom's GhostScript-derived EPS preview component to drop ROKRAT, KARAE, RICECURRY, or CHINOTTO. The detection fires when Hancom Office processes (Hwp.exe / Hwp32.exe / gbb.exe / gswin32c.exe) — or, in the more recent LNK delivery, an explorer-launched .lnk — spawn a script interpreter or download utility, a chain unique to South-Korea-targeted DPRK ops (G0067).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as child_path values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as sha256 from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("Hwp.exe","hwp.exe","Hwp32.exe","hwp32.exe","Hwordapp.exe","gbb.exe","gswin32c.exe","gswin64c.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","hh.exe","installutil.exe","msbuild.exe") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let _HancomParents = dynamic(["hwp.exe","hwp32.exe","hwordapp.exe","gbb.exe","gswin32c.exe","gswin64c.exe"]);
let _SuspChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","hh.exe","installutil.exe","msbuild.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_HancomParents)
| where FileName in~ (_SuspChildren)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256,
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessLogonId
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _HancomParents = dynamic(["hwp.exe","hwp32.exe","hwordapp.exe","gbb.exe","gswin32c.exe","gswin64c.exe"]);
let _SuspChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","hh.exe","installutil.exe","msbuild.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentName = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| extend ChildName  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,  @"\")[-1]))
| where ParentName in (_HancomParents)
| where ChildName  in (_SuspChildren)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, ParentName,
          NewProcessName, ChildName, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT37 ScarCruft Hancom Office HWP Spawning Script Interpreter
id: 9b1c4d22-6f5b-4d33-8c7e-1a2b3c4d5e60
status: experimental
description: Detects Hancom Office (HWP) processes or its embedded GhostScript components spawning PowerShell, cmd, mshta, rundll32, regsvr32, certutil or bitsadmin — the post-exploitation pattern for APT37 weaponised HWP/HWPX documents.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0067/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt37-overlooked-north-korean-actor
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g0067
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\Hwp.exe'
      - '\Hwp32.exe'
      - '\Hwordapp.exe'
      - '\gbb.exe'
      - '\gswin32c.exe'
      - '\gswin64c.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\hh.exe'
      - '\installutil.exe'
      - '\msbuild.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Internal Hancom macros that legitimately call cmd/PowerShell (rare; allowlist by hash)
level: high
ucs.rationale: HWP weaponisation is the single most actor-specific TTP in the APT37 playbook — the format is essentially South-Korea-only, and the CVE-2017-8291 / CVE-2018-4878 / EPS-preview chain that ScarCruft has burned through dozens of variants always lands the same way: Hwp.exe (or its bundled GhostScript components Hancom ships as gbb.exe / gswin32c.exe) becomes the parent of cmd, mshta, or PowerShell tha
